failure_modes.text: Conjunction-style optimism can make a risky plan look acceptable by understating how many dependent steps must all work.
failure_modes.mitigation: Decompose conjunctive assumptions: list every condition that must hold for the plan to work, estimate each independently, and multiply — if the compound probability is too low, the trade-off has been understated.

failure_modes.text: Motivated reasoning can make hard reallocations feel emotionally repulsive, so the team keeps its preferred project and hides the cost.
failure_modes.mitigation: Make reallocation concrete: after naming the trade-off, specify exactly what gets less funding, time, or attention — if nothing is cut, the trade-off is performative.

failure_modes.text: Trade-off talk can become performance without allocation, where everyone names the constraint but no one actually ranks or cuts anything.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Trade-Off Talk Becomes Performance Without Allocation
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate emotional attachment from economic value: when a project feels too important to cut, check whether the feeling is evidence-based or motivated reasoning protecting a sunk cost.

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?

heuristics.text: Upgrade pro-con talk to cost-benefit analysis so costs and benefits are weighed systematically instead of rhetorically.
Prioritize what matters most and explicitly name what not to do.
Hold conflicting perspectives long enough to ask what must be true before committing to one side of the trade-off.
heuristics.source_quote: Strategic thinking involves holding two or more seemingly conflicting data points or perspectives long enough to consider: "What must be true?"

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on explicit sacrifice, structured weighting, and anti-performative reallocation discipline, so the chunk set stays near those interventions.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor optimization language that widened beyond the raw source's more operational allocation and assumption-stress-testing guidance.
open_questions: The second and third premortem prompts come from the mitigation table's pause-and-deliberate guidance rather than from a labeled pre-mortem subsection.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on systems thinking, Pareto leverage, and clear intent for later relation preservation.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section is broader than the structured tension block and includes anchoring and availability-style distortions.
structured_tensions: The raw source explicitly preserves tensions against status-quo-bias and commitment-bias.
